Video.summary: When Justin and Meredith stay positive after a series of bad breaks, it pays off in fun. If fun means fish. After all, we are in Panama City Beach.
Video.title: "Positive = Good Day"

Video.summary: Establishing themselves just after the last Ice Age, the blacktail of Vancouver Island offers an important genetic population isolated from the mainland. To explore the local relationship with the species, its harvesting, conservation and history, Dan Cabela travels to the island.
Video.title: "The Blacktail of Vancouver Island"

Video.summary: As part of this year's Star brite Sea Safe event, we hit the water to experience some of the incredible fishing the Indian River Lagoon has to offer. For Rush, that meant some serious bow time with close friend Alex Hughey - on the hunt for some of the biggest snook we've ever encountered. The bite wasn't easy, but that didn't stop Rush from smashing his personal best - not once, but twice - in one unforgettable day on the water. Don't miss this action-packed episode of the Sea Safe adventure from beautiful Sebastian, Florida!
Video.title: "SeaSafe Snook Part 2"

Video.summary: We kick off Season 2 in Tennessee where we stop in Nashville, Townsend, and Johnson City!!
Video.title: "Traveling Over 2,000 Miles to Fish HERE!"

Video.summary: Mike heads to Table Rock Lake to meet up with Trenton and his family. Even battling the tough fishing conditions following a heavy front, there’s never a lack of family time, laughs or friendly competitions with the Southards.
Video.title: “Family Rock”

Video.summary: U.S. Marines combat correspondent Ken Plant tells of being captured and tortured as a POW.
Video.title: "Ken Plant"

Video.summary: A recap of this season's favorite fish!
Video.title: "Season 4 Wrap Up"

Video.summary: With human populations scattered through the landscape, human-wildlife conflict is a common occurrence in Borneo. Dan Cabela travels to the area to participate in an ambitious elephant collaring project, hoping to better understand how people and elephants can live harmoniously.
Video.title: "Human-wildlife conflict in Borneo"

Video.summary: Florida WaterSports Jeff Lange & Capt Justin head out of PCB in the wee morning hours hoping to land a limit of tasty table fare before the window of opportunity closes. Pro tip: Electric reels save shoulders.
Video.title: "We’re gonna need a bigger cooler."

Video.summary: On this episode, we travel north of the border for an unforgettable PART 1 Canadian fly-in fishing trip with Mandatory Fun Outdoors. We also highlight our next Scheels Hometown Hero, Jay Strande!
Video.title: "Canadian Fly-In With Mandatory Fun "Part 1""

Video.summary: On this episode, we travel to Walker, Minnesota to highlight a great organization tucked back in the woods called Camp Bliss! Camp Bliss hosts a wide arrange of events throughout the year that are tailored for Veterans to find rest, relaxation and respite. We tag along on an Ice Fishing outing, a turkey hunt and a Women's Veterans retreat as we highlight this great organization and the heroes they serve. We also highlight Des Moine Iowa SCHEELS hometown hero, Navy Veteran Steve Booth!
Video.title: "Camp Bliss"

Video.summary: On this adventure, we travel to Sioux Narrows Ontario Canada for a week long trip with Time on the Water to Lake of the Woods. In this episode we share the stories of Vietnam Veteran Leon Merhkens, fire fighters Jeff Benson/Seth Beyer and founder/CEO of Time on the Water, Chris Hansen. In addition, the fishing is pretty good too! We also highlight Iowa City Scheels Hometown hero Nic Pruter.
Video.title: "Time on the Water"

Video.summary: Motorcycle junkie and Army veteran Dave Allen recounts the terrifying prospect of facing chemical warfare during Operation Desert Storm with host Jay Garstecki and special guest Paul Sr. from American Choppers.
Video.title: "Dave Allen"

Video.summary: 82 year old Navy veteran with more than 2.7 million TikTok followers, Patriotic Kenny talks of how he helps other vets through him gifting mobility scooters to fellow veterans in need.
Video.title: "Patriotic Kenny"

Video.summary: U.S. Coast Guard helicopter pilot Shay Williams recounts his career flying countless search and rescue missions.
Video.title: "Shay Williams"

Video.summary: WWII U.S. Navy veteran Bill Kruschel recounts a journey from humble beginnings in the midst of the Great Depression to his valuable service in the Navy, culminating in a lifelong commitment to the field of special education.
Video.title: "Bill Kruschel"

Video.summary: An Honor No One Wants: A Gold Star Family is the immediate family member(s) of a fallen soldier who died while serving in a time of conflict. Kelli Campbell Goodnow honors the memory her husband…American hero Shawn Campbell.
Video.title: "Kelli Campbell - Goodnow"

Video.summary: Vietnam war veteran Don Weber recounts his amazing journey through survivors guilt, PTSD and starting over.
Video.title: "Don Weber"

Video.summary: U.S. Army veteran Carlos Powell tells of family sacrifice and fighting his post service “demons.”
Video.title: "Carlos Powell"

Video.summary: Capt. Mike joins Capt. Ozzie in Captiva, FL where the action is fast and furious and they chase down an inshore slam in this beautiful Florida fishery.
Video.title: "Captiva Slam"

Video.summary: Capt. Mike and Capt. Glenn Taylor fish their home waters and pay tribute to dear friend Capt. Billy Nobles.
Video.title: "Home Waters"

Video.summary: Capt Mike meets up with Leiza Fitzgerald and Jimmy Huddleston for a star tournament redfish tag mission.
Video.title: "Ozona Tag Team"

Video.summary: Long time friends Mike and Danny find a hot top water bite.
Video.title: "Horseshoe Beach"

Video.summary: Capt. Jonathan Moss is in Flamingo, Everglades National Park, in search of silver and gold.
Video.title: "Fiesta in Flamingo"

Video.summary: From Miami to Apalachicola Bay, Florida, Capt. Jonathan Moss revisits some of his favorite moments from this season.
Video.title: "From Bow to Stern"

Video.summary: Captain Jonathan Moss and Captain James Hillman are heading out of Highland Park Fish Camp in Deland, Florida, looking for largemouth bass.
Video.title: “Rustic Retreat”

Video.summary: Captain Jonathan Moss is fishing the South Carolina low country with Captain Stephen Flook Jr. The weather is cold and windy, but the plan remains the same, FULL SEND!
Video.title: "Full Send"

Video.summary: In part 1 of the British episodes, Andy starts his road trip around the United Kingdom in the Cotswolds where he sets out to hunt and taste the beautiful venison that Michelin star chef, Mark Robinson has to offer. He then proceeds to travel to the border of Scotland where he samples some of the finest and most historic bird shooting practices found throughout the British Isles.
Video.title: Wildbound - "UK Part 1"

Video.summary: A remnant of the last Ice Age, the musk ox is almost mythical in appearance. For the people who share the Barren Ground region of Canada with them, they are an integral part of their culture. Show host, Dan Cabela, participates in a scientific sampling project to monitor the - population's health, and get first-hand experience of the relationship between local people and this arctic icon.
Video.title: "Barren Ground Musk Ox"

Video.summary: Dan Cabela sets out to learn about the model of private ownership of wildlife in South Africa. Along the way, he participates in game capture, attends an auction and hunts to support a community.
Video.title: "Private Ownership of Wildlife"

Video.summary: Capt. Blair meets up with Capt. Ron Ryals to fish the phosphate pits of Bienville outdoors, an amazing spot for hunting and trophy bass catching.
Video.title: "Bassn' In Bienville"

Video.summary: Capt. Blair goes back to Marco Island with Capt. Chris Broome to tackle the beautiful inshore spots before heading offshore the next day.
Video.title: "Marco Island 2"

Video.summary: Capt. Blair Wiggins heads out the farthest he’s ever been offshore with Capt. Mike Avanon & Capt. Chris Broome in search of a species he’s never caught before.
Video.title: "Another One For The Bucket List"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by Florida Keys guide Jeff Malone to find a productive wreck with which they enjoy the exciting variety of fish that wrecks can provide.
Video.title: "Jeff Malones New Wreck"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by marine scientist Nick Castillo to search for bonefish in Key Largo for both fun and study.
Video.title: "Bonefish Science With Nick Castillo"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor take a trip to the Marathon, FL area to challenge some feisty sharks and sneaky snook.
Video.title: "Sharks And Snook Near Home"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by young guide Richard Black as he leads them to a backcountry slam.
Video.title: "Richard Black Slam"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by Key West guide Nick LaBadie, a talented guide with a special knack for finding permit on the Key West flats.
Video.title: "Key West with Nick Labadie"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by New York Cop Mike Counihan, who brings his passion for fitness and fishing along as the crew take on sharks in the Middle Florida Keys flats.
Video.title: "Mike Counihan And The Sharks"

Video.summary: Qualifier 2 for the BaitFuel Fishing Tour is back in Quinte West, Ontario where the teams will fish this Largemouth Bass Only event on the water of the Bay of Quinte.
Video.title: BaitFuel Fishing Tour - "Qualifier 2 - Quinte West, Ontario"

Image.alt: BaitFuel Fishing Tour - "Qualifier 2 - Quinte West, Ontario"
BaitFuel Fishing Tour - "Qualifier 2 - Quinte West, Ontario"
BaitFuel Fishing Tour - "Qualifier 2 - Quinte West, Ontario"

Video.summary: JP takes his largemouth flipping technique to the mangroves of Florida Bay.
Video.title: "Flipping the Salt"

Video.summary: As the SoCal Bluefin Tuna fishing season begins to take shape, Ali and Rush head out to fish the waters of San Diego and see what new surprises may be in store for them this year. For many boats in the fleet, the heading turns to the south for a visit to the commercial tuna pen operation that often attracts other fish from miles away. It's parking lot fishing at its best, but with a change in technique and break from the crowd Ali puts Rush on the bite!
Video.title: "Bluefin Season GO"

Video.summary: When it comes to fishing and the marine industry, we are often more united by the Bluefin tuna than we think. For Ali Hussainy, the Bluefin is responsible for fueling his life long passion as an angler, and in this episode he gets to share that passion with two friends that have their own ties to the species. For wildlife biologist Forrest Galante and master sushi chef Hiroyuki Terada, catching a bluefin tuna on rod and reel will be a first!
Video.title: "United By Tuna"

Video.summary: The Local Knowledge crew heads to Catalina Island off the coast of Southern California to participate in the 2024 War Heroes On Water tournament. This event offers a unique opportunity for the sport fishing community to create meaningful connections with our nation's heroes while raising awareness of the challenges they sometimes face when transitioning back to civilian life. With a little bit of luck and a lot of teamwork, Ali and Rush hope to put their veterans on a Bluefin tuna bite they won't forget!
Video.title: "War Heroes On Water"

Video.summary: Rush takes up an invite from good friend and fellow guide Jared Cyr to sight fish for barracuda and bonefish in the shallow waters of the Florida Keys. Meanwhile Rush's boat takes a break from charter fishing to get a new Seakeeper RIDE system installed.
Video.title: "Flats Ride"

Video.summary: Everyone knows Colorado for its epic mountains and fresh powder. But on this episode of DIRT, we’re digging into the unexpected side of the state (UFO rumors and all). Whether surfing infinite river waves, cultivating perfect carrot seeds, or fly fishing in downtown Denver’s shadow—geeky passions run high. Follow along as we gather ingredients for a farm to table final feast equal parts foraged, free-range, and free-thinking.
Video.title: DIRT - "Is Colorado a Food Utopia? Exploring Farm to Table Cuisine, Epic Landscapes & UFO's"

Image.alt: DIRT - "Is Colorado a Food Utopia? Exploring Farm to Table Cuisine, Epic Landscapes & UFO's"
DIRT - "Is Colorado a Food Utopia? Exploring Farm to Table Cuisine, Epic Landscapes & UFO's"
DIRT - "Is Colorado a Food Utopia? Exploring Farm to Table Cuisine, Epic Landscapes & UFO's"

Video.summary: In this episode we explore the wrecks and reefs that make Marathon one of the top dive destinations in the world. We will highlight what being done buy groups to restore our fragile coral reefs.
Video.title: "Marathon"

Video.summary: If you’ve ever heard, seen, or dreamed of the term van life, there is one man to thank for that, and his name is Foster Huntington. The filmmaker, photographer, and author has a storied career that includes the legendary book, "Home is Where You Park it". We met up with Foster (out on the road naturally) and he gave us a tour of his personal off-road camping rig he takes around the country.
Video.title: "How this Van Life Pioneer Set Up His Perfect Off-Grid Camper | Foster Huntington"

Image.alt: "How this Van Life Pioneer Set Up His Perfect Off-Grid Camper | Foster Huntington"
"How this Van Life Pioneer Set Up His Perfect Off-Grid Camper | Foster Huntington"
"How this Van Life Pioneer Set Up His Perfect Off-Grid Camper | Foster Huntington"
